session_hint: the Stripe webhook signature validation failure investigation
question: What did we conclude was causing Stripe webhook signature validation to fail?
turns: user
Stripe webhooks started failing signature validation yesterday around 14:00 UTC. All events rejected with 400. Customer subscriptions not updating.
user
Checked Stripe dashboard to see if our webhook signing secret was auto-rotated. It was not. Same secret in our secrets manager, unchanged.
user
Ruled out IP allowlisting — we do not restrict inbound IPs on the webhook endpoint. Not a network access issue.
user
Checked whether Stripe rate-limited us: dashboard shows no rate limit events, no 429s from Stripe's side. Ruled that out.
user

Root cause found: last Thursday we merged a PR that added express.json() middleware globally, including to the webhook route. Stripe computes its signature over the raw request bytes. Our middleware reads, parses, and re-serializes the JSON body before the webhook handler sees it. The re-serialized body has different whitespace and key ordering — the bytes differ from what Stripe signed. Signature mismatch is guaranteed on every request.
user
The fix is to bypass express.json() for the /webhooks/stripe route and use express.raw({ type: 'application/json' }) there instead, preserving the raw body.
user
Applied the fix to the webhook router. Deployed to staging and sent a test event from Stripe. Signature validates correctly.
user
Deployed to production. Webhooks processing cleanly. Replayed the 26-hour backlog of failed events via Stripe dashboard replay.
user
Confirmed secret rotation was not the cause — secret has been consistent throughout the incident.
user
Added an integration test that posts a Stripe-signed payload through the full Express middleware stack and asserts 200 OK. Closing the incident.
